entries.title: Harry lacks magical-world context before Hagrid

context.label: Before Hagrid reveals the wizarding world

content.fact: Before Hagrid explains the truth, Harry does not understand Hogwarts, wands, wizarding society, Voldemort, or his parents’ real world.
content.injection: Before Hagrid’s reveal, write Harry as isolated and uninformed about magic; do not give him Hogwarts vocabulary, wand competence, Voldemort context, or confident wizarding assumptions.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Hagrid reveals the wizarding world; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import later-book knowledge, deaths, relationships, or reveals into this Context.

content.fact: Harry, Ron, and Hermione become a real friend group only after the troll incident shifts Hermione from isolated classmate to trusted ally.
content.injection: Before the troll rescue, avoid writing the trio as already deeply bonded; after it, allow Harry, Ron, and Hermione to cooperate as friends while still retaining Year 1 immaturity.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.

entries.title: Flamel and Stone mystery not solved early

content.fact: After discovering Fluffy, the students know something is guarded but do not yet know the Philosopher’s Stone explanation or Nicolas Flamel connection.
content.injection: In the Fluffy mystery phase, keep the protected object uncertain; block confident Philosopher’s Stone or Flamel exposition until the Flamel identification anchor is reached. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.

entries.title: Quirrell/Voldemort truth hidden until final reveal

content.fact: Before the final confrontation, Quirrell’s role and Voldemort’s attachment to him are hidden, while Snape functions as a major misdirection.
content.injection: Before the Quirrell/Voldemort reveal, do not let Harry, Ron, Hermione, or ordinary narration know Quirrell is the threat; use suspicion of Snape and incomplete clues unless the story has changed the reveal.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
If the active Context is before this window, block this state unless accepted story lore explicitly changed it.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Before Quirrell/Voldemort reveal; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.

content.fact: After being removed from Gringotts, the Philosopher’s Stone is guarded at Hogwarts until the final confrontation resolves the plot.
content.injection: Do not treat the Stone as destroyed, safe, publicly known, or freely accessible before the final confrontation; before that point it remains a hidden protected object under Hogwarts defenses.
constraints: Use this as a story-position gate, not a general encyclopedia fact.
